name: Driftwood
text: The sea's last deliveries, still washing loose from the flats.

name: Wreck-Timber
text: Hull planking. Someone sailed this. Build gently.

name: Salt-Stiff Rope
text: Rigging that outlived its ship.

name: Salt
text: The one thing there is no shortage of.

name: Bronze Salvage
text: Fittings, nails, a ship's bell melted small.

name: Ship-Cloth
text: Sailcloth, faded to the color of the sky it worked under.

name: Coralwood
text: Timber from a forest where every tree is a dead animal.

name: Reef-Iron
text: Ore seeded through petrified coral. The Reefkin call it 'marrow' and would rather you didn't.

name: Pearl
text: The reef's last soft thing.

name: Anemone-Silk
text: Stinging thread, milked gently. It never learns you aren't food.

$comment: offeringValue 6 is invented — CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 2 names dye as 'cosmetic/offering' but gives no worship-rate number; set below the salt-votive (8) since it's a curiosity, not a devotional object.
name: Reef-Dye
text: Colors the flats never had. A spine ground fine enough to paint with.

name: Tidebrass
text: Metal from the first-age wrecks. It remembers being wet.

name: Storm-Glass
text: Sand fused by Maren's own hand. The priests trade sermons for it.

name: Trench Pearl
text: Grown in the dark by something that did not mean to make anything beautiful.

name: God-Water
text: Brinefolk-distilled. The best worship offering there is — mind how you came by it.

$comment: offeringValue 8 is invented (VILLAGER-AND-GODHEAD-SPEC Part III names beast keepsakes as things 'Ghal's altar craves' but gives no worship-rate number) — set below storm-glass's 12 since a keepsake is a grief-token, not a gathered treasure.
name: Shell-Plate
text: What's left when the shell outlasts what wore it. Old Ghal takes it gently, the way you'd take a photograph from a stranger's wallet.

$comment: offeringValue 8 is invented, same reasoning as item-shell-plate.
name: Hound-Fang
text: It never bit you. That was the whole point of it. You bury your dog by giving this to the god who kept it.

$comment: M3.b: the eel-wolf's keepsake, same reasoning + offeringValue band as item-hound-fang/item-shell-plate.
name: Eel-Fang
text: Curved for tearing, not holding. Old Ghal takes it the way he takes every fang — like it cost you something to bring it.

name: Crab Meat
text: Sweet, raw, and honest. Old Ghal is watching how you phrase that.

name: Smoked Crab
text: Salt and smoke and patience — a brinewife's answer to a hard day. Curing it fed Halor a little, too.

name: Dust-Eel Meat
text: Chewy, quick energy. Tastes like the flats smell.

name: Smoked Eel
text: The reef-runners swear by it: a coil of it in the pack and the horizon gets closer.

name: Urchin Meat
text: Sweet under all that armor. It never saw you coming and it still won't tell you how.

$comment: the B2 kitchen (CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 2): 'a step above Smoked Crab' — foodHp 40 > 35, foodStamina 30 > 25, foodMinutes 18 > 15.
name: Smoked Urchin
text: Cured low and slow. The reef gives better than the flats did, once you learn to ask it right.

name: Remnant of the Shell
text: A divine fragment — the endurance of the old sea, shelled in something you can hold. Enshrine it at a chapel, or consume it, and hear a warm voice approve.

name: Remnant of the Mother
text: A divine fragment — Old Ghal's watchfulness, grown enormous and hungry in the permanent dark below the canopy, and patient long enough to forget it was ever kind. Enshrine it at a chapel, or consume it, and hear a warm voice approve.

name: Verse of the Harpoon-Song
text: A fragment of an old whaler's song about a harpoon the Storm-Mother threw and lost. Three verses, they say, and the song teaches the forging. This one is yours now.

name: Maren's Own Harpoon

legend.history: She threw it once, at something that deserved it, and the storm has been looking for it ever since. Strike true and the bolt comes down on your mark.

text: A legend of the storm-line.

name: Neris's Hourglass

legend.history: Filled with the last wet sand in the world. Turn it and, once a day, ten seconds agree to happen again.

text: A legend of the tide-line.

name: The Salt-Father's Hearthstone

legend.history: A village heart. Set it in your hall and nothing within the walls spoils — food, stores, or resolve.

text: A legend of the hearth-line.

name: The Unlit Crown

legend.history: It costs what you'd expect. It gives more than you'd hope. That is how he works.

text: A legend of the dark-line.

name: The Lighthouse-Keeper's Lantern

legend.history: The keeper lit it the night the sea left, and swore it would burn until the water came back for it. He kept half the promise. The dark biomes respect it.

text: It never goes out.

name: Verse of the Keeper's Tale
text: A fragment of the old song the lighthouse-keepers sang to each other across the dark. Three verses, they say, and the song teaches the rite that keeps a lantern lit forever. This one is yours now.

name: Driftwood Club
text: The sea's first gift to anyone in trouble: a heavy piece of somewhere else's boat.

name: Bronze Knife
text: Salvage-bronze, workbench-ground. It held a ship together; it will hold you together.

name: Salt Votive
text: A small pressed cone of clean salt. The gods accept what the world has plenty of, if it is given with care.

name: Salt Ration
text: Cured, wrapped, dependable. The taste of getting home anyway.

name: Salt-Crust Cloak
text: Ship-cloth stiffened with salt until it turns a claw. Smells like the flats. Everything does.

name: Bronze-Scale Vest
text: Salvage-bronze sewn to a cloth backing, scale over scale. Heavy, honest, and it has stopped worse than a hound.

name: Bronze Spear
text: Reach, before the raiders learn you have it. Salvage-bronze on a driftwood haft, bound tight.

name: Bronze Mattock
text: A workbench-ground head on a good haft. Nothing in the Shallows needs it yet — the reef will. (Tool tier 2 — opens B2 nodes once the gather-gate lands at M3; inert for now.)

name: Ship-Cloth Bindings
text: Sailcloth wound tight and knotted the way a rigger knots a line — over the arms and chest, where a claw usually lands first.

name: Reef-Iron Blade
text: Forged at the reef-forge, from ore the Reefkin call marrow. It holds an edge better than bronze ever did.

name: Reef-Iron Pike
text: Slow and long — the eel-wolf's answer. It keeps the pack at a distance that favors you. (Slow/reach is flavor at EA; the number carries the weapon for now.)

name: Reef-Iron Mattock
text: A reef-forge head on a coralwood haft. Nothing in the reef needs it yet — the Drop will.

name: Anemone-Silk Wrap
text: Light, and it still stings a little on the inside — the anemone never quite forgets what it was for.

name: Reef-Iron Scale
text: Marrow-iron plates, overlapped like the coral that grew them. Heavier than bronze, and it earns the weight.

$comment: offeringValue 15 is invented (CRAFT-AND-BUILD-SPEC Part 2 names it 'Neris craves, Vessa accepts' but gives no worship-rate number) — set above storm-glass (12) since a pearl worked into a votive is scarcer than sand the sky fused for free.
name: Pearl Votive
text: A pearl set in coralwood, the way the flats used to set salt in bronze. Neris keeps it like she kept everything else — patiently, and for a long time.
